covariate_description: Number of packages delivered at the stop
interpretation: Each additional package multiplies service time by exp(coef)
notes: Most consistently reported covariate across literature

covariate_description: Total volume of packages in cubic metres
interpretation: Cube effect over and above package count — heavier/larger items
notes: Less consistently reported; some studies use weight instead

covariate_description: Binary: 1 if any package requires signature
interpretation: Signature adds fixed time for customer availability + paperwork
notes: Strong consistent positive effect across all studies

covariate_description: Binary: 1 if MDU/apartment (vs residential SFD baseline)
interpretation: MDU requires lobby navigation, lift, finding unit
notes: One of the largest effect sizes in the literature

covariate_description: Binary: 1 if commercial (vs residential SFD baseline)
interpretation: Commercial typically faster per package if someone present to receive
notes: Direction varies - can be positive if secured/staffed

covariate_description: Binary: 1 if CBD/dense urban (vs suburban baseline)
interpretation: CBD adds time for parking distance, lobby security, access
notes: Separate from stop type - geography effect independent

interpretation: Rural: easy access but potentially different building types
notes: USPS OIG key source for this covariate

covariate_description: Binary or continuous: peak morning period
interpretation: Morning deliveries faster - recipients more available; less fatigue
notes: Time-of-day effects documented but vary by network type

covariate_description: Binary: 1 if packages sorted to delivery sequence
interpretation: Our key model parameter - sorted packages reduce search/handling time at stop
notes: This is the core β for our model - currently synthetic at -0.12 log units

interpretation: Secured buildings, intercom, distant parking all add time
notes: Less commonly modelled explicitly; often absorbed into MDU/CBD dummies

covariate_description: Walking distance from vehicle park to door (metres)
interpretation: Direct walk time component - key in dense urban
notes: Urban Freight Lab Seattle papers key source for this
